prompt:
=== FARSHORE — RULES PRIMER ===

YOUR ROLE
  You command at the strategic level. You form task forces and give each an
  objective ("Task Force 1: secure the eastern sea"). You do NOT move
  individual units, choose individual targets, or resolve combat — subordinate
  officers carry out your intent and report back. If you find yourself naming a
  unit's destination tile, you've dropped too low.

VICTORY
  You win when the enemy owns zero cities (a player with no cities cannot build
  and is finished). Only cities build. A neutral city can be captured by an
  army like any other city; it then builds for you — taking neutrals does not
  win the game, but each one adds production.

UNITS
  name        moves-on  HP  spd  str  carries      builds-in
  ARMY        land       1    1    1   —             5 turns
  FIGHTER     air        1    8    1   —            10   (range 20; must refuel)
  PATROL      sea        2    4    1   —            15
  DESTROYER   sea        3    3    2   —            20
  SUBMARINE   sea        2    2    3   —            25
  TRANSPORT   sea        3    2    0   6 armies     30   (str 0: cannot fight)
  CARRIER     sea        8    2    1   8 fighters   40
  BATTLESHIP  sea       18    2    4   —            50
  SATELLITE   orbit      1    1    0   —            50   (recon only; cannot attack or be attacked; 50-turn life)

MOVEMENT & TERRAIN
  - Land units move on land + cities; they cannot enter water.
  - Sea units move on water + cities; they cannot cross land, and must sail
    around it. Cities are the only tiles where land and sea units meet.
  - A unit moves up to its speed at full HP; damaged units move less. A unit
    regains HP only on a turn it does not move.
  - One unit per tile (exception: cargo, below).

COMBAT  (the engine resolves it; you only weigh it)
  Probabilistic. Higher strength and higher current HP win more often; either
  side can lose HP or be destroyed. Strength-0 units (transport, satellite)
  never fight — a transport caught by an enemy warship is destroyed.

SEA TRANSPORT  (how armies cross water — and what is expected of you)
  An army can never enter water. Transports carry them: a transport loads up
  to 6 armies at a city or coastal tile, sails, and unloads onto adjacent
  land. YOUR OFFICERS DO ALL OF THIS. If a task force contains armies and a
  transport and you give it an objective across water, the officers march
  the armies to the transport, load, sail, escort, unload, and press the
  objective — you never order the rendezvous, the loading, or the landing.
  Expected use: put the armies, the transport, and (if the sea is not safe)
  a warship into ONE task force and name the objective. A transport has
  strength 0 — alone it is a target, not a warship; a warship in the same
  task force escorts it automatically. Never STAGE land units on water.
  Cargo is kind-locked — transports carry armies only, carriers carry
  fighters only; ships never carry ships. If a loaded transport or carrier
  is destroyed, everything aboard is lost with it.

CAPTURING CITIES
  Only an army captures a city, by entering it. Capture is a 50% roll, and a
  successful capture consumes the army (it garrisons the city). A city can take
  several armies to secure, and each attempt spends one.

PRODUCTION
  No currency. A city builds ONE unit at a time; it finishes after that unit's
  build-time in turns, then starts the next. Switching what a city builds
  DISCARDS all accumulated progress — effort toward one unit does not transfer
  to another.

AIR & SPECIAL
  - Fighter: must reach a FRIENDLY city or a carrier within its range (20) or
    it is lost. It cannot land on an enemy city.
  - Satellite: reveals everything within scan radius 10 — terrain AND any enemy
    units currently there — but far-reaching and deep in enemy territory. It
    only sees; no other effect, cannot be shot down.

VISION / FOG
  You see within any of your units'/cities' scan radius (army 2, sub 1,
  patrol/destroyer/battleship 3, transport 2, carrier 4, fighter 5, sat 10).
  You remember terrain you've seen, but an enemy unit is known only where you
  can see right now — remembered enemy positions may be stale.

TURN 58  (your last orders were issued as shown per TF)

MAP  legend: . land  ~ water  ? fog   O my city  E enemy city  N neutral city
     a-r = your units, see UNITS (a unit inside a city shows as the city)
     X Y = enemy units in sight NOW (see KNOWN ENEMY)
 r0  . h O i . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r1  . j b . N . ~ ~ ~ p c E ? ?
 r2  . O . . . . ~ ~ ~ q d E . ?
 r3  . . . k O . ~ ~ ~ r e f g ?
 r4  . . . m . . ~ ~ ~ Y ? ? ? ?
 r5  ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?

UNITS  (map marker · id · kind · position · tasking) — this is your
ENTIRE force; you have NOTHING else
  a  #1   army       (2,0) in city                TF-2
  b  #2   army       (2,1)                        TF-2
  c  #4   army       (10,1)                       TF-5
  d  #5   army       (10,2)                       TF-5
  e  #6   army       (10,3)                       TF-5
  f  #7   army       (11,3)                       TF-5
  g  #8   army       (12,3)                       TF-5
  h  #11  army       (1,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  i  #12  army       (3,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  j  #13  army       (1,1)                        UNASSIGNED
  k  #14  army       (3,3)                        UNASSIGNED
  l  #15  army       (4,3) in city                UNASSIGNED
  m  #17  army       (3,4)                        UNASSIGNED
  n  #18  army       (2,0) in city                UNASSIGNED
  o  #19  army       (4,3) in city, NEW t57       UNASSIGNED
  p  #9   transport  (9,1) empty                  TF-5
  q  #16  transport  (9,2) empty                  TF-5
  r  #10  destroyer  (9,3) escorting              TF-5
MY CITIES
  (2,0) building ARMY, 2 turns left
  (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 26 turns left
  (4,3) building ARMY, 4 turns left
NEUTRAL CITIES  (4,1) on my continent
KNOWN ENEMY
     city (11,2)
  X  enemy army garrisoning city (11,1)
  Y  enemy destroyer at (9,4), sighted t58, near my empty transports

CURRENT TASKINGS  (standing orders; your stated reason in quotes;
  events since, as reported; members are in UNITS above)
  TF-2  formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"
    since: garrisoned; no contact; no losses
  TF-5  formed t54 · CAPTURE (11,1) — "strike east and take the enemy cities with full lift and escort"
    since: landed t56, six armies ashore;
      t57: capture attempted — failed, #3 lost; the enemy army holds (11,1);
      t58: enemy destroyer sighted at (9,4), near my empty transports

TASK: amend your standing orders. Output ONLY lines in these forms — no other
prose, headers, or commentary:

  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
  TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>
  FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>

Every standing TF gets exactly one line — a DISBAND line IS that TF's one
line. FORM lines are additional lines creating new TFs; they do not count as
any standing TF's line. To change a TF's membership, DISBAND it and FORM
anew; DISBAND releases surviving members to UNASSIGNED. UNASSIGNED units
only enter play through FORM. A BUILD line is optional per city — no BUILD
line means the city keeps its current build (changing discards accumulated
work). VERB is one of:
  CAPTURE <city (x,y)> · DEFEND <city (x,y)> · SCOUT <(x,y) or compass> ·
  PATROL <(x,y) or compass> · STAGE <(x,y)>
A warship grouped with a transport escorts it — there is no ESCORT order.

message.content:
TF 2: CONTINUE | "maintain capital garrison"
TF 5: RETASK CAPTURE (11,2) | "seal enemy production and secure city E"
message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to play the "FARSHORE" game, commanding strategic units and task forces without micromanaging individual combat or movement (that's left to officers).
I need to output commands based on the provided state of the world at Turn 58.

**Current State Analysis:**
*   **My Forces:**
    *   TF-2: Armies #1, #2 at city (2,0) garrisoned. Objective DEFEND. No contact.
    *   TF-5: Formed t54, Objective CAPTURE (11,1). Has armies #3-8 and transports #9, #10 (empty?), destroyer #16 (wait, unit `r` is destroyer at 9,3, unit `p` is transport at 9,1, `q` is transport at 9,2). Wait, looking at the list:

        *   q (#16) - transport (9,2) empty.

        *   Unit `X Y = enemy units in sight NOW`.

        *   My Transports are at (9,1) and (9,2). The Destroyer is at (9,3). Enemy Destroyer is at (9,4). They are close.
        *   TF-5 Objective is CAPTURE (11,1). "landed t56... capture attempted... failed". It seems TF-5 has been bogged down or lost progress.
        *   Armies #3-8 are likely near the transport/escort group based on TF-5 formation logic, but looking at the list: `c` (#4), `d` (#5), `e` (#6), `f` (#7), `g` (#8) are all under TF-5.
        *   Wait, the list says "p #9 transport (9,1)", "q #10 transport (9,2)"? No, unit IDs are numbers. `p` is ID 9, `r` is ID 10. The text above lists:
            *   c #4 ... TF-5
            *   d #5 ... TF-5
            *   e #6 ... TF-5
            *   f #7 ... TF-5
            *   g #8 ... TF-5
            *   p #9 transport (9,1) empty TF-5
            *   q #16 transport (9,2) empty TF-5 (Wait, `q` is listed as unit 16?) No, the list has letters for IDs. Let's assume `p`=`#9`, `r`=`#10`. But the list says `r #10 destroy...`. And `q #16 transport`.
            *   Okay, let's parse units carefully based on the "UNITS" section text:
                *   c (#4) TF-5
                *   d (#5) TF-5
                *   e (#6) TF-5
                *   f (#7) TF-5
                *   g (#8) TF-5
                *   p (#9) transport (9,1) empty TF-5
                *   q (#16) transport (9,2) empty TF-5 -> Wait, ID 16? And `r` is #10 destroyer. This is inconsistent numbering or ID vs name mixup in the prompt? Ah, letters a-m-o correspond to numbers. `a=1`, `b=2`, `c=4`.
                *   Okay:
                    *   c #4
                    *   d #5
                    *   e #6
                    *   f #7
                    *   g #8
                    *   p #9 (Wait, 'p' is the 16th letter, but ID is 9?) No, 'a'=1, 'b'=2, ... 'p'=16.
                    *   Let's check `r`: 'r' is unit #10 in the text? "r #10 destroyer". If a=1..o=15, then p=16. So r would be 17. But it says #10. This suggests IDs might not map directly to letters or there's a typo in the prompt's provided units list vs my understanding.
                    *   Let's look closer: "c #4", "d #5", "e #6", "f #7", "g #8".
                    *   Then "p #9", "q #16", "r #10"? That implies IDs are scattered. I should treat the numbers as the IDs.

                    *   Locations: Transports at (9,1) and (9,2). Destroyer at (9,3). Enemy Dest at (9,4). Enemy Army at (11,1).

I need to secure my transports or move them closer to friendly land/cities for safety, especially with the Destroyer in danger. But TF-5's objective is CAPTURE (11,1). The map shows water surrounding these coordinates (9,4 to 11,1 seems like open sea/water ~ based on `~` in r3/r4 rows? No, `(9,4)` is `Y`, which is enemy destroyer. `(11,2)` is city E. `(11,1)` has enemy army X. Row `r3` has water around (9,1) area. Wait, `.` is land, `~` is water.
                    *   Looking at map:
                        *   r1: ... b . N . ~ ~ ~ p c E ? ?
                            *   Col 9 is 'p' (Transport #9). Col 10 is 'c'? Wait `c` is army #4. Map shows `c` at (10,1)?
                            *   Text says: `c #4 army (10,1)`. Map Row 1 has `~ ~ ~ p c E ? ?`. If `p` is col 9, `c` is col 10?
                        *   r2: ... . . . k O . ~ ~ ~ q d E . ? -> No, let's map the rows to coords.
                        *   Rows are x,y? Or y,x? The text says "r1... r5". Usually Row 1 is top (smaller Y?), but coords usually (x,y).
                        *   Let's check: `a #1 army (2,0) in city`. Map `r0`: ... h O i . `. Wait, r0 corresponds to y=0? `h` is at (1,0)? `i` is at (2,0)? No.
                        *   Map row 0: `. h O i .`. If x=0..5? This looks like ASCII art map where each char is a tile.
                        *   Let's rely on the text list coordinates primarily.
                            *   c #4 (10,1), d #5 (10,2), e #6 (10,3), f #7 (11,3), g #8 (12,3). All TF-5.
                            *   p #9 transport (9,1). q #16 transport (9,2). r #10 destroyer (9,3).

                        *   Map Legend: `.` land, `~` water.

                        *   Anyway, TF-5 has Armies 4-8 (5 armies) and Transports 9, 16, Destroyer 10.
                        *   Goal: CAPTURE (11,1).
                        *   Enemy Defense at (11,1). Capture attempt t57 failed (#3 lost - wait, unit list shows no #3 currently listed? List has c(4)..g(8)). Ah, the prompt says "t57: capture attempted — failed, #3 lost". This implies I had unit #3 in TF-5. It's gone now.
                        *   Now we have 5 armies (4,5,6,7,8).
                        *   Enemy has army at (11,1) and destroyer at (9,4). My transports/destroyer are vulnerable at (9,1)-(9,3). The enemy destroyer is adjacent to my convoy. I need to either hide the convoys or push hard while they are near a friendly city (or just move them safely).
                        *   TF-2: Holding (2,0). "keep the capital garrisoned". No action needed unless threatened.
                        *   Production: (2,0) building Army. (1,2) building Transport. (4,3) building Army.

*   **Decision Making:**
    1.  **TF-5**: The current objective is CAPTURE (11,1). But the convoy/transport situation is bad. The Enemy Destroyer (#9,4) is near my Transports/Destroyer cluster (#9,3). If I don't move, they get sunk. However, TF-5 has Armies 4-8 on land around x=10-12? Wait, if they are on land (x=10,11,12), and my transports are at (9,1), (9,2). Why were they unloaded there?
        *   Ah, `p` (#9) is Transport. Armies 4-8 are at (10,1)-(12,3). Are they on land or sea? If they are "armies", they can't move in water.
        *   Wait, the text says: "d #5 army (10,2)". Map `r1` has `p c`. `c` is likely at (9,1) based on proximity to `p`? No, coords matter.
        *   Crucial detail: "Sea Transport ... sails, and unloads onto adjacent land."
        *   If Armies are at (10,2) etc., they are likely in a port city or safe zone.
        *   Wait, TF-5 is listed with Transports `p(9,1)` and `q(9,2)` as members.
        *   Objective: CAPTURE (11,1).
        *   Enemy Destroyer at (9,4). My Destroyer at (9,3).
        *   This looks like a "run" situation. I need to push my transport force forward or consolidate near a city before the enemy destroyer hits it.
        *   However, TF-2 needs no change.
        *   Wait, "City (1,2) building TRANSPORT". That is at x=1, y=2. Far from conflict?

        *   TF-5 is the main offensive force.
        *   The Enemy Destroyer at (9,4) is a threat to my assets in the sea.
        *   If I don't order TF-5 to move/army, they risk being destroyed or losing more time if they can't secure (11,1).
        *   Actually, if my Army #4, 5, 6, 7, 8 are on land (e.g. (10,1), etc.), they are garrisoned? No, `TF-2` is "keep the capital garrisoned". TF-5 is active.

        *   The immediate threat is my empty transports at (9,1) and Destroyer at (9,3). They need to be secured or used effectively. If they are needed to move troops, I should use them immediately.
        *   Enemy has no visible army in TF-2 area.

But the rule says: "If a task force contains armies and a transport and you give it an objective across water...". This implies I need to stage troops.

        *   Wait, looking at `r0`: `. h O i .`. `O` is my city (2,1? No `(2,0)`).
        *   Map: r0 is likely row 0. `a #1 army (2,0) in city`. `O` usually means my city. `h` is at (1,0)? `i` is at (3,0)?
        *   TF-5 Armies are listed at x=10+.
        *   If they are ashore on land, they can't be moved by transport unless we are moving them to a city.
        *   The prompt says: "You win when the enemy owns zero cities".
        *   I should likely try to push my force to secure (11,2) or nearby friendly nodes to build more troops/defend against the destroyer threat.
        *   However, TF-5 is already trying to capture (11,1). T57 failed. #3 lost.
        *   If I RETASK or CONTINUE. CONTINUE implies no major change.
        *   If the enemy destroyer is there and my convoy/destroyer is weak (#10 Dest vs 9,4 Dest), and transports are empty at (9,1). The enemy might attack them if I don't move fast.
        *   But I can't order individual units to run or load. I assign a Task Force an objective.
        *   If the TF-5 is stuck, maybe RETASK.
        *   However, if I RETASK, I have no time lost on movement (since I don't command).
        *   Let's look at Production. My cities are building: Army (2,0), Transport (1,2), Army (4,3).
        *   I can order a build. `BUILD (x,y): UNIT`. The rules say "A BUILD line is optional per city — no BUILD line means the city keeps its current build". If I don't change it, it stays.
        *   So I should just give orders to TFs.
        *   Wait, do I need to move the empty transports? They are at (9,1), (9,2). Destroyer is at (9,3). Enemy Dest is at (9,4). This is a dangerous water zone. My destroyers might be engaging or I should get them out of there or use them for attack.
        *   Actually, since the enemy army garrison is at (11,1), and my Armies are nearby (listed as part of TF-5), maybe the transports should move to a city to unload, or the Armies need to press capture.

        *   If I keep CONTINUE, the enemy destroyer might finish me off on the next turn.
        *   Given I am a strategic commander, if my assets are exposed, I should order a re-positioning or objective change that mitigates risk. But "move units" is not for me. I assign objectives like "PATROL", "CAPTURE".
        *   If I RETASK to CAPTURE (11,2)? That's the Enemy City E. But I am targeting (11,1).
        *   Maybe RETASK to DEFEND (something else)? No, need to capture cities.
        *   Best move: TF-5 is stuck/losing momentum against garrison at (11,1) and exposed transports. I should probably use the Transport force more effectively or just let officers deal with it while focusing production?
        *   Wait, if I can't command loading/unloading, maybe I should RETASK to a target where my forces are safer? Or maybe the Transports need to carry armies further.
        *   Actually, the prompt asks for "amend your standing orders".
        *   TF-2: DEFEND (2,0). Seems stable. Keep as is. `TF-2: CONTINUE`.
        *   TF-5: Needs fixing. It has transports and armies near enemy. The Enemy Destroyer is threatening. If I RETASK to capture (11,1), it's the same objective but maybe with more context? No, "CONTINUE" is valid for no change in objective.
        *   But my situation is tactically precarious. "Enemy destroyer sighted... near my empty transports". If the destroyers fight each other, I win nothing but lose time/assets.
        *   Wait, "If a task force contains armies and a transport... YOUR OFFICERS DO ALL OF THIS." The officers will load and sail.
        *   So if I say RETASK TF-5 CAPTURE (11,2), the army is already on land (4-8). Why are transports empty there?
        *   Maybe I should use a new Task Force with the trans/esc to push deeper or defend?
        *   Wait, I can only have one order per standing TF. DISBAND and FORM requires breaking the old one. If I keep TF-5, I must issue one line.
        *   Given the risk of my Destroyer (#10) being hit by Enemy Dest #9-4 (adjacent coords 9,3 vs 9,4), and Transports empty... if they don't leave the water zone immediately, they are vulnerable.
        *   However, I can't order "Move". I have to pick an objective that drives them off the coast or into a safe harbor.

        *   Maybe the risk is that my empty transports are sitting there and will be sunk if I don't order them to move or defend. But I can't order "Defend". I must pick VERB: CAPTURE, DEFEND, SCOUT, PATROL, STAGE.
        *   If I RETASK TF-5: STAGE (11,1)? That implies staging troops there.
        *   Wait, look at the units list again. Armies 4-8 are under TF-5. Transports 9,16 and Dest 10 are under TF-5.
        *   Are my armies on land or sea? Army #4 is (10,1). Water `~` starts around column 7+. So x=10 might be water if the map is narrow? No, land/sea distinction: "Cities and adjacent lands". "Sea units move on water... cannot cross land".
        *   If my army is at (10,1) and Transport is at (9,1), they are adjacent. If (10,1) is Land, the Army is safe. The Transports in the water need to go somewhere.
        *   If I RETASK TF-5: CAPTURE (11,2)? No, that's Enemy City E. Capturing it makes it mine and stops them from building there. That's a priority victory condition. "Enemy owns zero cities".
        *   So, shifting TF-5 to capture the visible enemy city at (11,2) (City E on map r1?) could be better. (11,1) is neutral/land garrisoned by enemy army? It says "garrisoning city (11,1)".
        *   If I RETASK TF-5 CAPTURE (11,2), the officers might move my armies towards there. But wait, if I capture (11,1), that's closer. (11,2) is E.
        *   Maybe RETASK to DEFEND (11,1)? No, I need to win cities.
        *   Actually, given the exposed transports, maybe I should use TF-5 to secure (11,1) but prioritize getting troops into cities first?
        *   Wait, there is a transport at (9,2). If I RETASK to SCOUT (some direction)? No, need to capture.
        *   Let's look at `TF-2`. No action needed. `CONTINUE`.
        *   `TF-5` is the problem. The enemy destroyer at (9,4) is a direct threat to my convoy/destroyer cluster at (9,1)-(9,3). I need them out of that spot or to fight aggressively. If I don't act, they might get sunk by an "expected" outcome if not protected. But I have a warship in the same TF (#10). It should escort my transports.
        *   The prompt says "A warship grouped with a transport escorts it".
        *   So TF-5 is set up to protect them? "Enemy destroyer... near my empty transports". Maybe they haven't met yet. Or enemy is fast.
        *   Given the risk and that I've already failed capture of (11,1), I should switch focus. Capture (11,2) is a direct threat removal for me (enemy city).
        *   RETASK TF-5 CAPTURE (11,2)? Or CONTINUE? If I keep objective (11,1), I am still in the water with exposed assets. Capturing (11,2) forces movement to secure land.
